Requisitos La última acción del onryo fue un Golpe de garra con éxito

Efeto El onryo trata de instilar su necesidad de venganza en la criatura. Con su toque, lanza un @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Q690d3mw3TUrKX7E]{Geas} de 5.º rango a la criatura a la que Golpea (@Check[will|dc:30]). Con un fallo, se verá forzada durante una semana a acabar con los seres queridos del malhechor del onryo y a cualquiera que intente defenderlos. Con un fallo crítico la duración es de 1 mes y la criatura obtiene Rencor de onryo, que también está forzada a utilizar por el geas. Sin importar el resultado, la criatura queda inmune temporalmente al Rencor de onyro de ese individuo durante 1 año.

action->Vengeful Suffocation.name: Asfixia vengativa
action->Vengeful Suffocation.description:
Frecuencia una vez por hora

Efecto El onryo canaliza su resentimiento sin límites, manifestándolo como un cúmulo enredado de aire húmedo, un grumo rancio de fango de pantano u otra masa de materia asquerosa, en la garganta de una criatura hasta a 30 pies (9 m). La criatura debe hacer una salvación de @Check[fortitude|dc:32].

Éxito crítico La criatura no sufre ningún efecto.

Éxito La criatura queda @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Indispuesta 1}.

Fallo La criatura queda @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Indispuesta 2}.

Fallo crítico La criatura queda @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Indispuesta 3} y la masa tapona completamente sus vías respiratorias. La criatura empieza a ahogarse .

Pale Sovereign.name: Soberano pálido
Pale Sovereign.publicNotes:
Un soberano pálido es una rara forma de muerto viviente que se alza de una maldición terrible que afecta únicamente a los de sangre real. Esta maldición drena la vitalidad de su víctima lentamente con el tiempo, casi pareciendo una enfermedad degenerativa que ninguna medicina puede curar. La realeza afligida por la maldición enferma y se marchita hasta finalmente perecer, alzándose como soberano pálido tras su muerte. La apariencia de un soberano pálido es similar a la que tenía en vida, pero tan mortalmente pálido y demacrado que nadie que resista los efectos que nublan la mente de su aura podría pensar que están vivos.

La maldición se conoce con diversos nombres, el Debilitamiento, el Legado Marchito, la Heráldica Desmadejada, pero sus efectos son los mismos. Tras sufrir la maldición, los miembros de un linaje real empiezan a morir, disminuyendo sus descendientes hasta solo quedar uno. Al convertirse en el último miembro de su linaje, el infortunado sufre la transformación en soberano pálido, el potencial y poder de todo su linaje canalizado en una criatura muerta viviente extremadamente peligrosa, incluso aunque el rey careciera de relativa importancia o habilidad en vida.

Los métodos para infligir la maldición varían. En general es el resultado de rituales ocultistas que requieren un lanzador de conjuros de gran poder. A veces la maldición se usa contra un linaje real que abusa de su autoridad, pero igual de a menudo la usan fuerzas malignas contra gobernantes benévolos o simplemente por despecho del lanzador de conjuros que la lanza. Mencionada solo en susurros y veladas alusiones, la maldición es muy temida entre las familias reales que la conocen. Sé cauto en tus tratos, dicen los rumores, y ten cuidado con quien desdeñas, puesto que nunca sabes qué poder puede tener.

En raras ocasiones, un soberano pálido puede alzarse de forma espontánea si el último de su linaje muere bajo una maldición o debido a una maldición, especialmente si murió con tareas pendientes o como resultado de una traición. Cualquier maldición sirve en estas raras circunstancias, y es posible que el primer soberano pálido se formará así.

Aunque malhumorados y melancólicos, los soberanos pálidos son normalmente neutrales y no malignos. No obstante, son muy protectores con sus territorios y cualesquiera súbditos que permanezcan en ellos, y atacar el territorio es la forma más rápida de atraer su ira. Si un soberano pálido es expulsado del trono central de su país, se retirará a otra posesión cualquiera que aún tenga y pueda gobernar.

Las actitudes de los súbditos de los soberanos pálidos varían. Muchos no son siquiera conscientes de la auténtica naturaleza del gobernante. Entre los que conocen el secreto, algunos valoran la protección del soberano y lo aprecian. Otros están incómodos con un monarca muerto viviente eterno, especialmente si el soberano se ha vuelto corrupto o amargado.

El territorio de un soberano pálido está marcado de forma indeleble por su presencia. Las plantas se marchitan y mustian, luchando por crecer en un suelo seco y sin vida. Los animales salvajes a menudo se alzan como muertos vivientes a menos que reciban rituales de entierro especiales, llevando a grupos de bestias esqueléticas y zombificadas vagando por el territorio. Tales monstruos no dañan a los habitantes del lugar, pero sí atacan prontamente a cualquier intruso que no lleve el sello del monarca. Incluso los habitantes vivos del territorio de un soberano pálido suelen tener una apariencia drenada y agotada, como si estuvieran a un paso de la tumba.
Pale Sovereign.saveDetails: -2 por estatus a todas las salvaciones contra maldiciones

action->Demesne Confinement.name: Confinamiento en el dominio
action->Demesne Confinement.description: Un soberano pálido está atado a su dominio, un territorio de un radio de 5 millas (8 km) alrededor de su propiedad. No puede abandonarlo por voluntad propia. Un soberano pálido sacado de su dominio no puede usar ninguno de sus conjuros innatos o rituales y se marchita gradualmente en el transcurso de una semana, convirtiéndose en polvo si no vuelve antes de que pasen 7 días.

action->Sense Visitors.name: Sentir visitantes
action->Sense Visitors.description: Un soberano pálido sabe automáticamente cuándo una criatura entra o sale de su dominio, aunque eso no le otorga el conocimiento preciso de la ubicación de esta.

action->-2 to All Saves vs. Curses.name: -2 por estatus a todas las salvaciones contra maldiciones

action->Aura of Charm.name: Aura de encanto
action->Aura of Charm.description: 80 pies (24 m). El aura de un soberano pálido es seductora y atractiva, afecta a todas las criaturas que inician su turno en ella con un @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.vLA0q0WOK2YPuJs6]{Hechizar} de 8.º rango (salvación @Check[will|dc:39]). Una criatura que falla su salvación ve al soberano como era en vida, no su actual forma muerta viviente. Una criatura que tiene éxito en su salvación queda inmune temporalmente al aura de dicho soberano durante 24 horas.

action->Curse of Eternal Sleep.name: Maldición del sueño eterno
action->Curse of Eternal Sleep.description:
El soberano pálido toca a una criatura viva adyacente para hacer que caiga en un sueño eterno. El objetivo debe hacer una salvación de @Check[will|dc:39].

Éxito crítico La criatura no sufre ningún efecto y queda inmune temporalmente durante 24 horas.

Éxito La criatura queda soñolienta y @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Lentificada 1} durante 1 minuto.

Fallo La criatura queda @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Inconsciente}, aunque no queda @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Tumbada} ni suelta lo que estuviera agarrando. No puede despertarse con una prueba de Percepción, aunque causar cualquier cantidad de daño rompe el efecto. Si sigue inconsciente después de 1 hora, se despierta.

Fallo crítico La criatura queda inconsciente permanentemente, permaneciendo tumbada y no puede despertarse en tanto la maldición perdura..

action->Siphon Vitality.name: Absorber vitalidad
action->Siphon Vitality.description: El soberano pálido absorbe la fuerza vital de las criaturas que lo rodean. Todas las criaturas en una @Template[emanation|distance:30]{emanación de 30 pies (9 m)} sufren @Damage[6d8[void]|options:area-damage]{6d8 daño del vacío} (salvación @Check[fortitude|dc:39|basic|options:area-effect]). El soberano pálido recupera tantos PG como el daño mayor que sufre una criatura debido a este efecto.

Pale Stranger.name: Pale Stranger
Pale Stranger.languages: any one language spoken in life by their murderer (typically common)
Pale Stranger.publicNotes:
A pale stranger arises when a gunslinger is slain in a duel by an underhanded foe. Buried with their pistols, the corpse claws out of the grave to wreak vengeance upon their killer for cheating. Sometimes a pale stranger is created when a particularly petty gunslinger is beaten fairly and refuses to accept defeat, though such an individual must be especially self-delusional to retain the hatred necessary to spur the transformation. Whatever the circumstance, a pale stranger can wield their firearms with deadly grace and efficacy.

A revenant is a vengeful undead who stalks the one who killed them in life. They're relentless in the pursuit of their murderer, seeking a final justice.

action->Self-Loathing.name: Self-Loathing
action->Self-Loathing.description:
If a pale stranger sees their own reflection or any object important to them in life, they must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:30] save.

Critical Success The pale stranger is unaffected and can no longer be affected by that reflection or object in this way.

Success The pale stranger is distracted by self-loathing and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Lentificado 1} for 1 round.

Failure The pale stranger becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinado} by the source of their self-loathing and does everything they can to destroy it until the end of their next turn.

Critical Failure The pale stranger becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Inmovilizado} as long as the source of their self-loathing is apparent, until they're attacked, or until they see their murderer.

action->Sense Murderer.name: Sense Murderer
action->Sense Murderer.description: A pale stranger knows the direction of their murderer (as long as both are on the same plane), but not the distance.

action->Undying Vendetta.name: Undying Vendetta
action->Undying Vendetta.description: If the pale stranger's murderer dies, the pale stranger is immediately destroyed. A pale stranger that can't sense their murderer must attempt a @Check[type:flat|dc:11] once every 24 hours to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Inmovilizado} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Tumbado}; they immediately rise again once they can sense their murderer. A murderer who becomes undead does not trigger the pale stranger's destruction until the murderer is finally destroyed. The pale stranger gains a +2 status bonus to checks and DCs against their murderer.

action->Bullets of Vengeance.name: Bullets of Vengeance
action->Bullets of Vengeance.description: Shots from a pale stranger's firearms deal an additional 1d6 precision damage, or 2d6 precision damage against the one who murdered them. A creature who has never killed a living being doesn't take this precision damage. When a pale stranger critically succeeds at a ranged Strike with a pistol, the target must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:29] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Aturdido 1}.

action->Dead Shot.name: Dead Shot
action->Dead Shot.description:
Frequency once per turn

Requirements The pale stranger is wielding two loaded firearms

Effect The pale stranger takes aim at a target and shoots both of their firearms simultaneously. The pale stranger makes two ranged Strikes, one with each of the firearms, each using the pale stranger's current multiple attack penalty. Both Strikes must have the same target, who must be within the first range increment of both weapons.

If both attacks hit, combine their damage, and then add any other applicable effects from both weapons. The pale stranger adds any precision damage only once, to the attack of their choice. Combine the damage from both Strikes and apply resistances and weaknesses only once. This counts as two attacks when calculating the pale stranger's multiple attack penalty

action->Revenant Reload.name: Revenant Reload
action->Revenant Reload.description: The pale stranger reloads each firearm they're wielding, supernaturally generating new black powder and bullets. When firing these magical bullets, the firearm can't misfire, and the attack doesn't trigger reactions triggered by ranged attacks.

Phantom Footsteps.name: Phantom Footsteps
Phantom Footsteps.description: Audible footsteps approach from behind, but their source is not apparent.
Phantom Footsteps.disable: @Check[type:religion|dc:17|name:Ward Off Lesser Spirits] (trained) to ritually ward off lesser spirits or @Check[type:occultism|dc:18|name:Excorcise the Spirit] to exorcise the spirit

action->Stalk.name: Stalk
action->Stalk.description:
Trigger A creature passes by the footsteps' path

Effect Footsteps with no visible source closely shadow the triggering creature, then stop. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:16] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Asustado 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Asustado 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Asustado 3}.

Phantom Jailer.name: Phantom Jailer
Phantom Jailer.description: A spirit appears and arrests the creature by clamping manacles around their wrists.
Phantom Jailer.disable: @Check[type:athletics|dc:18|name:Seize the manacles] to seize the manacles from the spirit, @Check[type:intimidation|dc:19|name:Order the spirit to stand down] (trained) to order the spirit to stand down, or @Check[type:occultism|dc:20|name:Exorcise the spirit] to exorcise the spirit

action->Capture.name: Capture
action->Capture.description:
Trigger A creature touches the manacles

Effect The spirit attempts to lock the manacles around the triggering creature's wrists. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[type:reflex|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The spirit loosely places manacles around the creature's wrists. Removing the manacles requires a single action.

Failure The spirit clamps the manacles around the creature's wrists but fails to lock them. The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Desprevenido} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Torpe 1} until the manacles are removed as a 3-action activity.

Critical Failure The spirit clamps the manacles around the creature's wrists and locks them. The creature is off-guard and clumsy 1 until the manacles are removed (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} DC 23, simple lock).

Polong.name: Polong
Polong.languages: any one language known by the polong's creator
Polong.publicNotes:
Polongs exist to serve their creators as loyal familiars, spies, and assassins. Their convenience makes them popular among the wicked, as each resides within a glass bottle which normally seems to contain only a small amount of red liquid. When addressed by their owner, a polong appears within their bottle as a tiny, beautiful humanoid drenched in blood. Forcing one to manifest outside the bottle reveals their true shape: a blood-soaked spirit with a hateful visage and sickle-shaped claws.

The ritual to create a polong originated in Minata and is known primarily by Tian-Sing spellcasters, but only those willing to practice such evil. The ritualist must collect the blood of a murdered humanoid in a small bottle and perform daily incantations, which can take up to 14 days. Once the polong forms, they drink the blood of their creator every day, most often taken from a finger inserted into their bottle. At the master's order, a polong will travel up to 10 miles away to locate a particular person, possess them, and either slowly whittle them down via possession until they die, or force them to attack anyone nearby until someone slays the victim in self-defense.

action->Bottle Bound.name: Bottle Bound
action->Bottle Bound.description: A polong is bound to the bottle from which they spawned. They can leave the bottle only at the will of their master and must return to it once every 24 hours to feed on their creator's blood. Otherwise, the polong withers and dies.

action->Natural Invisibility.name: Natural Invisibility
action->Natural Invisibility.description: A polong is naturally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} outside of their bottle. They become visible only when forced out of a possessed creature against their will. Shattering the polong's bottle also forces them to assume a visible form.

action->Regenerating Bond.name: Regenerating Bond
action->Regenerating Bond.description: When a polong possesses a creature, the polong's master gains temporary Hit Points equal to the victim's level × the victim's drained value caused by the possession. These temporary Hit Points last as long as the possession lasts.

action->Polong Possession.name: Polong Possession
action->Polong Possession.description:
The polong attempts to possess an adjacent corporeal creature. This has the same effect as the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wU6hNzK8Yfqdmc8m]{Possession} spell (@Check[type:will|dc:26]), except the duration is 24 hours and, since the polong doesn't have a physical body, they're unaffected by that restriction of the spell. If the target has critically failed its save and become fully possessed by this polong before, it gets a save result one degree of success worse than it rolled.

A creature possessed by a polong becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drenado 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Condenado 1}. These conditions don't reduce naturally unless the polong spends an entire day without possessing the creature. For each consecutive day the polong is able to return and possess the creature, the drained and doomed values each increase by 1, to a maximum of 4. If the polong possesses a different creature, the conditions end for any creature it previously possessed.

Predatory Rabbit.name: Predatory Rabbit
Predatory Rabbit.publicNotes:
Skittish in life, predatory rabbits are fast and nimble predators in unassuming forms. They most enjoy hunting birds, squirrels, cats, and other rabbits.

Bonds of loyalty that endure beyond death reanimate pets to crawl from their graves within days of dying, returning to the side of their past owners, family, or pack. Unfortunately, death irrevocably changes a risen pet. They return as aggressive, predatory beasts, difficult to control and easy to anger. Even the most friendly and docile of pets becomes sadistic and violent in undeath—fixating first on those it views as competition for the attention of its owners. When a risen pet is jilted, rebuffed, or offended by its owner, it turns on them.

action->Sense Companion.name: Sense Companion
action->Sense Companion.description: A predatory rabbit knows the direction of its past owner or family (as long as they're both on the same plane), but not the distance.

action->Stench.name: Stench
action->Stench.description: 10 feet. A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:13] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Indispuesto 1} (plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Lentificado 1} for as long as it's sickened on a critical failure). While within the aura, affected creatures take a -2 circumstance penalty to saves against disease and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

action->Feral Leap.name: Feral Leap
action->Feral Leap.description: The predatory rabbit jumps with a maximum height and distance each equal to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions. At any point, the predatory rabbit attempts a jaws Strike.

Priest Of Kabriri.name: Sacerdote de Kabriri
Priest Of Kabriri.publicNotes:
Un gran número de seres poderosos han competido por la posición de patrón de los muertos vivientes, pero ninguno de ellos es tan venerado entre los guls como el señor demoníaco @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.deities.Item.V5PbbkZUP9N7kl6h]{Kabriri}, El Que Roe. Casi todos los guls que sienten alguna inclinación por venerar a los dioses adoran a Kabriri, que es honrado a través de rituales profanos y ceremonias macabras centrados en el consumo glotón de seres inteligentes. Presidiendo dichas liturgias están los sacerdotes de Kabriri, guls particularmente viles y astutos que han sido elegidos específicamente por su primogenitor con el deber de asegurarse de que su progenie jamás pase hambre.

Se puede encontrar a los taimados y astutos guls en cualquier lugar en el que hay cadáveres sepultados, tratando de satisfacer su inagotable hambre de carne humanoide.

action->Ecstatic Ululation.name: Aullido de éxtasis
action->Ecstatic Ululation.description:
Trigger A ghoul ally within 30 feet Consumes Flesh to regain Hit Points

Effect The priest of Kabriri unleashes a rapturous howl in praise of their god. All ghouls within 30 feet of the priest of Kabriri, including the priest but not the triggering ghoul, regain half as many Hit Points as the triggering ghoul. All living creatures within 30 feet take void damage equal to half the number of Hit Points the triggering ghoul gained, with a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:20|basic:true]. Only one Ecstatic Ululation can affect a use of Consume Flesh. Any beyond the first have no effect.

action->Consume Flesh.name: Consumir carne
action->Consume Flesh.description:
Requisitos El sacerdote de Kabiri está adyacente al cadáver de una criatura que ha muerto en la última hora

Efecto El sacerdite de kabiri devora un pedazo del cadáver y recupera @Damage[3d6[healing]]{3d6 Puntos de Golpe}.

Puede recuperar Puntos de Golpe una sola vez por cadáver.

action->Ghoul Fever.name: Ghoul Fever
action->Ghoul Fever.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.N7UV5CZXtcoxDxCF]

Saving Throw @Check[type:fortitude|dc:20]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect (1 day)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d6[void]] damage and regains half as many Hit Points from all healing (1 day)

Stage 3 as stage 2 (1 day)

Stage 4 @Damage[2d6[void]] damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day)

Stage 5 as stage 4 (1 day)

Stage 6 dead, and rises as a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary.Actor.LHHgGSs0ELCR4CYK]{Ghoul} the next midnight.

action->Paralysis.name: Paralysis
action->Paralysis.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.FW4KAUHb7r8WkxUc]

Any living, non-elf creature hit by a priest's jaws or claws Strike must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:20] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralizado}. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save.

action->Swift Leap.name: Swift Leap
action->Swift Leap.description: The priest jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

Provincial Jiang-shi.name: Provincial Jiang-shi
Provincial Jiang-shi.hpDetails: fast healing 10, void healing, one more breath
Provincial Jiang-shi.publicNotes:
Jiang-shi are terrifying hopping vampires from Tian Xia. Though similar to other vampires—they are undead, drain vital energy from the living, and can be slain only in specific ways—jiang-shi feast upon the breath of the living, also known as qi or ki, instead of blood. While they possess unique powers, including their magic-deflecting paper fulus, extraordinary jumping ability, and immunity to sunlight, jiang-shi also possess unique vulnerabilities, such as the inability to see living creatures save through breathsense, a revulsion to bells and mirrors, and near-stupefying melancholia. Thankfully, most jiang-shi can't create vampiric spawn.

Roused rudely from their nightmarish slumber, the provincial jiang-shi roams the villages and graveyards around their domain searching for sustenance to slake their hunger.

action->Drain Qi.name: Drain Qi
action->Drain Qi.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.QMlQ5AvcunCvjlfM]

Requirements A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Agarrado}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralizado}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Neutralizado}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Inconsciente}, or willing creature is within the jiang-shi's reach

Effect The jiang-shi drains the victim's life energy, or qi, through their breath. This requires an @Check[type:athletics|defense:fortitude] check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drenado 1} and the jiang-shi regains [[/r 13[healing]]]{13 HP}, gaining any excess as temporary Hit Points. Draining qi from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP to the jiang-shi but increases the victim's drained condition value by 1, killing the victim when it reaches drained 5. If the victim has the ability to cast ki spells, it can spend 1 Focus Point to avoid becoming drained. This protects it only from the current attack, not from subsequent attempts to Drain Qi.

A victim's drained condition value decreases by 1 every week. If the creature restricts their diet to glutinous rice for at least 1 day and spends 10 minutes dancing, jogging, or otherwise engaging in vigorous physical activity by succeeding at a @Check[type:athletics|dc:25] check, it reduces its drained condition by 1.

Prowler Wight.name: Prowler Wight
Prowler Wight.publicNotes:
Often found lurking in the dark, prowler wights wield subterfuge as their greatest weapon. They frequently feign death in old sewers, graveyards, and mausoleums, waiting for hapless victims to pass by before striking.

Wights are undead beings of spite who primarily stalk places related to death.

action->Final Spite.name: Final Spite
action->Final Spite.description:
Trigger The prowler wight is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The prowler wight makes a Strike before being destroyed. They don't gain any temporary HP from drain life on this Strike.

action->Drain Life.name: Drain Life
action->Drain Life.description: When the hunter wight damages a living creature using an unarmed attack or their bound weapon, they gain 9 temporary Hit Points, and the creature must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:25] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drenado 1}. Further damage dealt by the hunter wight's unarmed and bound weapon attacks increases the value of the drained condition by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4.

action->Feign Death.name: Feign Death
action->Feign Death.description: The prowler wight quenches the telltale red glow in their eye sockets, falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Tumbado}, and lies completely still. Until the next time they act, the prowler wight appears to be an ordinary corpse. They have an automatic result of 38 on Deception checks and DCs to pass as an ordinary corpse.

action->Sneak Attack.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.AWvNPE4U0kEJSL1T]

The prowler wight deals an additional 2d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Desprevenido} creatures.

action->Spawn Prowler Wight.name: Spawn Prowler Wight
action->Spawn Prowler Wight.description: A living humanoid killed by a prowler wight's weapon or claw Strike rises as a hunter wight spawn after [[/br 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. This spawn is under the command of the prowler wight that killed them. They don't have drain life or spawn prowler wight and are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Torpe 2} for as long as they're a prowler wight spawn. If the creator of the prowler wight spawn dies, the prowler wight spawn becomes a fully autonomous prowler wight; they regain their free will, gain drain life and spawn prowler wight, and are no longer clumsy

Queen Sluagh.name: Queen Sluagh
Queen Sluagh.publicNotes:
A queen sluagh grows more powerful by devouring souls collected from underlings or drained directly through her proboscis. The muscular tongue inside is tipped with a cartilaginous, barbed quill that feeds the queen not only the soul, but the strengths of its owner. A queen conceals her nest deep in a secluded area, such as a forest or graveyard.

A soul about to leave its physical body gives off a peculiar scent, usually unnoticed by mortal creatures. But the undead fey called sluagh can smell and track down its location with frightening ease. Unlike most fey, sluagh are undead, possessing strong connections to void energy and mortal souls. Sluagh collect the souls of those close to death and bring them to feed their queen.

Most sluagh appear bald-headed, with rounded ears, sharp teeth, glossy black eyes, and scrawny bodies. Their rib cages show through their skin, which is translucent and soft as velvet.

Sluagh can become ghostly and incorporeal for brief periods. They use this power to stalk their prey to great effect. Sometimes the glimpse of a sluagh is mistaken for a ghost sighting.

spell->Visions of Danger (A Swarm of Slu.name: Visions of Danger (A Swarm of Sluagh Reapers)

action->Frightful Presence.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.etMnv73EIdEZrYYu]

120 feet. @Check[type:will|dc:37]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.FrightfulPresence]

action->Soulscent (Imprecise) 200 feet.name: Soulscent (Imprecise) 200 feet
action->Soulscent (Imprecise) 200 feet.description: The sluagh is aware of all doomed creatures, dying creatures, and creatures that died within the last hour within the listed range.

action->Voice of the Soul.name: Voice of the Soul
action->Voice of the Soul.description: A sluagh queen knows any language of a creature whose soul they've devoured and can perfectly mimic its voice by attempting a Deception check to [[/act lie options=voice-of-the-soul]], with a +4 circumstance bonus.

action->Call of the Damned.name: Call of the Damned
action->Call of the Damned.description: The queen sends out shriek to all sluagh within 1 mile, summoning them to her. This typically calls [[/r 2d4]] @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.book-of-the-dead-bestiary.Actor.uURI8Netd0ytD9vc]{Sluagh Reapers}. If the queen is facing foes strong enough to threaten her, these are likely too weak to pose a threat but can assist by fetching items or getting in enemies' way.

action->Eat Soul.name: Eat Soul
action->Eat Soul.description: The queen touches a dying creature or a creature that died within the past 1 hour with her proboscis. If the target is dying, it must attempt a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:41] save; on a failure, its dying value increases by 2 (3 on a critical failure). If the target is dead, the queen devours the soul. Unless the queen is destroyed, the target can't be returned to life through any means, even powerful magic such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.6dDtGIUerazSHIOu]{Wish}.

action->Ghostly Swoop.name: Ghostly Swoop
action->Ghostly Swoop.description:
The queen sluagh becomes incorporeal until the start of their next turn, and Flies up to their fly Speed. While incorporeal, they are immune to precision damage, and have resistance 10 to all damage (except force, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.JQdwHECogcTzdd8R]{Ghost Touch}, or vitality); this resistance is doubled against non-magical damage.

After using Ghostly Swoop, the queen sluagh can't use it again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Ghostly Swoop]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Soul Theft.name: Soul Theft
action->Soul Theft.description:
Requirements The queen has a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Agarrado} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Neutralizado} with her proboscis

Effect The queen devours a portion of the creature's soul and steals some of its ability. The creature takes @Damage[5d8[void]] damage (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:41] save). The queen gains temporary HP equal to the total amount of HP the target loses, including any HP lost from being drained.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drenado 1}.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is drained 1 and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Condenado 1}. The queen steals a spell slot or training. If she steals a spell from a prepared caster, she transfers the spell to herself; if she steals from a spontaneous caster, she gains a prepared spell of the appropriate rank and expends one of the creature's spell slots of that rank. If she steals training, she chooses Perception, attack modifier, or any skill. For 24 hours, she gains a +2 status bonus to that statistic and the creature takes a -2 status penalty. The queen can have any number of stolen abilities, but only one from each creature.

Critical Failure As failure, but double damage.

Raw Nerve.name: Raw Nerve
Raw Nerve.publicNotes:
When a living creature is killed by massive amounts of electricity (such as by being struck by lightning or subjected to a magical attack), their dying terror sometimes causes their soul to linger on the Material Plane. Necromantic energy takes hold of their brain and nervous system, causing these organs to burst forth from the charred flesh and become a raw nerve. These horrific undead float a few inches off the ground in a macabre arrangement morbidly parodying the creature's living form, with its brain at the top and nerves dangling below, crackling with electrical energy.

A raw nerve can sense the thoughts of the living, perceiving them as a chaotic jumble that evokes an uncontrollable rage. The undead seeks to destroy the sources of these thoughts, which often means killing every living creature it can sense. Once a raw nerve detects no more thoughts, it grows still, floating in an almost meditative state. However, its fury instantly reawakens should a thinking creature pass too close. Though it's not mindless, a raw nerve can't be reasoned with, and the only way to stop one is to destroy it.

action->Thoughtsense 60 feet.name: Thoughtsense 60 feet
action->Thoughtsense 60 feet.description: The raw nerve senses all non-mindless creatures at the listed range.

action->Accelerating Inquest.name: Accelerating Inquest
action->Accelerating Inquest.description: The raw nerve mentally probes a creature within 100 feet, attempting an @Check[type:occultism|defense:will] check against the creature's Will DC. On a success, the raw nerve can Step, and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against that creature until the start of the raw nerve's next turn.

action->Float.name: Float
action->Float.description: A raw nerve floats a few inches from the ground. As such, it can ignore difficult terrain.

action->Involuntary Reaction.name: Involuntary Reaction
action->Involuntary Reaction.description: A creature struck by a raw nerve's nerve ending strike must succeed at a @Check[type:reflex|dc:26] saving throw or be unable to use reactions until the start of its next turn.

action->Synaptic Overload.name: Synaptic Overload
action->Synaptic Overload.description:
The raw nerve releases a burst of mental energy in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30], dealing @Damage[4d12[mental]] damage to each creature in the cone (@Check[type:will|dc:23] save).

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Aturdido 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Aturdido 2}

Relictner Eroder.name: Vestigiador erosionador
Relictner Eroder.publicNotes:
Sin importar su edad, los vestigiadores parecen enanos ancianos con profundas arrugas, escaso cabello cano y formas decrépitas. Conforme se degrada su cuerpo, también lo hace lo que lo rodea, con incluso la mejor arquitectura desmoronándose en ruinas.

Los mortics son humanoides abrumados por la energía del vacío que se aferran tenazmente a la vida, manteniéndose en la delgada línea entre la vida y la muerte.

action->Consecration Vulnerability.name: Vulnerabilidad a la consagración
action->Consecration Vulnerability.description: Un vestigiador que está en una zona de culto dedicada a una dios no maligno o en suelo sagrado, como un área bendecida por @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.y3jlOfgsQH1JjkJE]{Terreno santificado}, queda @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Lentificado 1}.

action->Lair Sense.name: Sentir guarida
action->Lair Sense.description: El vestigiador se vincula con una estructura que considera su hogar, como una casa, un templo o unas ruinas. Es consciente de cualquier criatura que entra en su guarida, y la dirección y distancia aproximada de esta. Si es destruida, el vestigiador puede vincularse con una nueva guarida residiendo en una estructura que posea o reclame durante 1 semana.

action->Weathering Aura.name: Aura de marchitamiento
action->Weathering Aura.description: 60 pies (18 m). El vestigiador irradia un aura de degradación. Todos los objetos en el área ven reducida su Dureza en 10, hasta un mínimo de 0, en tanto permanecen en el aura y durante 1 asalto tras abandonarla. El vestigiador no puede desactivar esta aura.

action->Crumble.name: Desmoronarse
action->Crumble.description: El vestigiador erosiona un objeto dentro de una emanación de 30 pies (9 m), causándole @Damage[3d10[bludgeoning]]{3d10 daño contundente}, con una salvación @Check[reflex|dc:31|basic] si el objeto está siendo atendido.

action->Death Gasp.name: Bocanada de muerte
action->Death Gasp.description:
El vestigiador da una gran bocanada de aire y la aguanta, suspendiendo temporalmente sus procesos biológicos y convirtiéndose en muerto viviente. El vestigiador obtiene los rasgos de muerto viviente y se vuelve inmune a dormir, efectos de muerte, enfermedad, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Parálisis}, sangrado y veneno. Cualquier efecto de este tipo que le está afectando en este momento queda suspendido hasta que vuelve a respirar.

Bocanada de muerte dura mientras el vestigiador contiene la respiración (hasta 12 turnos)

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.lyeRL7khixdlJsaf]{Efecto: Bocanada de muerte}

action->Demolition.name: Demolición
action->Demolition.description:
El vestigiador ruge con furia, causando que la estructura que le rodea se derrumbe. Todas las criaturas en una @Template[emanation|distance:30]{Emanación de 30 pies (9 m)} sufren @Damage[8d10[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage]{8d10 daño contundente} por los cascotes (salvación @Check[reflex|dc:31|basic|options:area-effect]). Una criatura que falle su salvación queda @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Tumbada}. El área se convierte en terreno difícil.

El vestigiador no puede utilizar demolición nuevamente en [[/gmr 1d4 #Recarga Demolición]]{1d4 asaltos}.

Runecarved Lich.name: Liche rúnico
Runecarved Lich.publicNotes:
En su búsqueda incansable de poder mágico, algunos liches utilizan sus formas putrefactas para albergar secretos mágicos, ocultando preciosos tomos en sus costillas, inscribiendo fórmulas en su carne marchita e incluso reemplazando algunos huesos con poderosos objetos mágicos. El proceso es muy doloroso, puesto que la jaula del alma trata de reparar cualquier daño causado por semejantes alteraciones.

El deseo de vivir eternamente motiva a un lanzador de conjuros a convertirse en liche, y el conocimiento nigromántico hace realidad su deseo. El liche típico toma la ruta más directa, manteniendo su forma muerta viviente bastante similar a su apariencia en vida. Pero la muerte en vida también otorga mayor libertad a aquéllos con una visión menos convencional de en lo que se pueden convertir. Los liches horda y los liches rúnicos se reforman completamente a sí mismos.
Runecarved Lich.saveDetails: +1 por estatus a todas las salvaciones contra vitalidad

equipment->Wand of Manifold Missiles (7t.name: Wand of Manifold Missiles (7th-Rank Spell)
equipment->Wand of Manifold Missiles (7t.description:
This wand features a carved dragon's head at its top and a polished metal sphere set in its midsection.

Activate Cast a Spell

Frequency Once per day, plus overcharge

Effect You cast @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gKKqvLohtrSJj3BM]{Force Barrage} at 7th rank.

After you cast the spell, an additional missile or missiles are released from the wand at the start of each of your turns, as though you cast the 1-action version of magic missile. Choose targets each time. This lasts for 1 minute, until you're no longer wielding the wand, or until you try to activate the wand again.

Craft Requirements Supply a casting of magic missile of the appropriate rank.

consumable->Wand of Wall of Force (Level.name: Wand of Wall of Force (Level 6)
consumable->Wand of Wall of Force (Level.consumableSpellDescription:
You form an @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} wall of pure magical force up to 50 feet long and up to 20 feet high. The wall has no discernible thickness. You must create the wall in an unbroken open space so its edges don't pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost. The wall has AC 10, Hardness 30, and 60 Hit Points, and it's immune to critical hits and precision damage. The wall blocks physical effects from passing through it, and because it's made of force, it blocks incorporeal and ethereal creatures as well. Teleportation effects can pass through the barrier, as can visual effects (since the wall is invisible). Wall of force is immune to counteracting effects of its level or lower, but the wall is automatically destroyed by a disintegrate spell of any level or by contact with a rod of cancellation or sphere of annihilation.

Heightened (+2) The Hit Points of the wall increases by 20.
consumable->Wand of Wall of Force (Level.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.kiXh4SUWKr166ZeM]

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.7Iela4GgVeO3LfAo]{Wall of Force}

This baton is about a foot long and contains a single spell. The appearance typically relates to the spell within.

Activate Cast a Spell

Frequency once per day, plus overcharge

Effect You Cast the Spell at the indicated level.

Craft Requirements Supply a listed-level casting of the spell.

Note: To create a scroll or wand of a specific spell, drag the spell from the compendium or compendium browser into the inventory of a PC, NPC, or loot actor.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Vital.name: +1 por estatus a todas las salvaciones contra vitalidad

action->Frightful Presence.description:

60 feet. @Check[type:will|dc:38]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.FrightfulPresence]

action->Rejuvenation.name: Rejuvenecimiento
action->Rejuvenation.description: Cuando un liche es destruido, su alma se transfiere inmediatamente a su @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.NEbr7nKuiluJgBHT]{Jaula del alma. Un liche puede ser destruido permanentemente solo si se encuentra su jaula del alma y se la destruye.

action->Spellstealing Counter.name: Contra robaconjuros
action->Spellstealing Counter.description:
Desencadenante Una criatura a la que el liche puede ver Lanza un conjuro

Requisitos El liche rúnico tiene menos de tres conjuros almacenados en su tomo

Efecto El liche gasta un espacio del mismo nivel que el conjuro que está siendo lanzado para contrarrestar el conjuro de la criatura desencadenante. El liche rúnico pierde su conjuro como si hubiera lanzado el conjuro desencadenante. El liche trata de luego de contrarrestar el efecto desencadenante (modificador a contrarrestar [[/r 1d20+35#Counteract]]{+35}). Si tiene éxito el conjuro es contrarrestado y se añade al tomo del liche rúnico.

action->Stored Items.name: Equipo almacenado
action->Stored Items.description:
El liche rúnico puede almacenar objetos en su cuerpo, como se detalla más abajo. El liche puede Interactuar para retirar o almacenar un objeto. Si el cuerpo del liche es destruido, cualquier objeto almacenado aparece entre sus huesos.

  • Arma almacenada (extradimensional) El liche puede almacenar una única arma a una mano, de Impedimenta 1 o menor, unida mediante runas especiales talladas en los huesos de uno de los brazos del liche. Este liche rúnico tiene el hacha de batalla entre sus objetos, pero otro individuo diferente podría tener otra arma. Un arma que esgrime el liche causa un daño adicional igual a la mitad del número de dados de su ataque de mano.
  • Tomo de conjuros Un liche rúnico tiene entre sus costillas un tomo cuidadosamente creado, lleno de poder mágico. Este tomo puede albergar hasta tres conjuros robados por la aptitud Contra robaconjuros.
  • Varita de brazo (extradimensional) El liche rúnico puede almacenar hasta dos varitas en el cúbito y radio de uno de sus brazos. Estas varitas pueden usarse como si las empuñara y no se las puede desarmar. Si una varita de brazo se sobrecarga, el liche sufre @Damage[20[untyped]]{20 daño} si la varita se rompe o @Damage[40[untyped]]{40 de daño} si se destruye.

action->Consult the Text.name: Consultar el texto
action->Consult the Text.description:
Frecuencia una vez al día

Efecto El liche rúnico hace que se abran las páginas de su tomo de conjuros, desvelando un conjuro de ocultismo de nivel 9.º o inferior a su elección. Durante el resto del día, el liche puede lanzar dicho conjuro como si fuera uno de sus conjuros conocidos de cualquier nivel desde la base hasta 9.º

action->Drain Spell Tome.name: Drenar el grimorio
action->Drain Spell Tome.description: Efecto El liche extrae la energía mágica almacenada en su libro de conjuros para lanzar uno de los conjuros que ha contrarrestado. El conjuro es del mismo nivel que el que ha contrarrestado, pero utiliza la CD del liche rúnico y su ataque de conjuros.

action->Steady Spellcasting.name: Lanzador de conjuros seguro
action->Steady Spellcasting.description: Si una reacción interrumpiría la acción de Lanzar un conjuro del liche, éste hace una @Check[flat|dc:15]. Con un éxito la acción no se ve interrumpida.

action->Summon Weapon.name: Convocar arma
action->Summon Weapon.description:
Requisitos El liche tiene un arma almacenada en su brazo y esa mano libre

Efecto El liche rúnico convoca su arma almacenada a su mano (la del brazo donde almacena el arma).

Sadistic Conductor.name: Sadistic Conductor
Sadistic Conductor.description: A phantom conductor appears with a flourish, causing spectral instruments with razor-like strings to appear in the hands of those present. As the conductor gestures for the performance to begin, creatures are compelled to play the instruments.
Sadistic Conductor.disable: @Check[type:perfomance|dc:18] to perform so well the conductor releases you from your instrument, banishing it voluntarily; @Check[type:occultism|dc:22] (trained) or @Check[type:religion|dc:22] (trained) to banish one instrument; or @Check[type:thievery|dc:25] (trained) to sabotage one instrument; each instrument must be banished, sabotaged, or destroyed to disable the haunt
Sadistic Conductor.reset: The haunt deactivates when all conjured instruments are destroyed or there has been silence for 1 minute. It resets after 1 hour.
Sadistic Conductor.routine: (1 action; enchantment, mental, occult) The conductor urges each creature within 30 feet to play the hazardous instrument it holds. Each creature that is compelled to play its instrument does so, and those who succeeded on their save can choose to do so. Playing the instrument, either from the haunt's routine or of a creature's own volition during an attempt to disable the haunt, deals @Damage[(1d6+3)[slashing]] damage (@Check[type:reflex|dc:21|basic:true|traits:enchantment,mental,occult] save), and the creature must attempt a @Check[type:performance|dc:18|traits:enchantment,mental,occult,damaging-effect] check. On a success, the conductor accepts the performance and dismisses that creature's instrument, and on a critical failure, the discordant sound and conductor's jeers deal an additional @Damage[(1d6+3)[sonic]] damage.

action->Conjure Instruments.name: Conjure Instruments
action->Conjure Instruments.description:
Trigger A creature approaches within 15 feet

Effect The conductor conjures a hazardous spectral instrument into the hands of each sentient creature within 30 feet. Even a creature whose hands are full finds a spectral instrument superimposed over the other items it's carrying. Affected creatures must each attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:19] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The conjuration fails and no instrument appears in the creature's hands.

Success A conjured instrument appears in the creature's hands, but it isn't compelled to play it.

Failure A conjured instrument appears in the creature's hands and it's compelled to play it. The creature can't willingly put down the instrument while compelled to play.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature needs a critical success on its Performance in order for the conductor to be satisfied and dismiss its instrument.

Scorned Hound.name: Scorned Hound
Scorned Hound.publicNotes:
Scorned hounds rise from the most devoted dogs. They're incredibly possessive of their owners, allowing no living beings to approach and refusing to be parted.

Bonds of loyalty that endure beyond death reanimate pets to crawl from their graves within days of dying, returning to the side of their past owners, family, or pack. Unfortunately, death irrevocably changes a risen pet. They return as aggressive, predatory beasts, difficult to control and easy to anger. Even the most friendly and docile of pets becomes sadistic and violent in undeath—fixating first on those it views as competition for the attention of its owners. When a risen pet is jilted, rebuffed, or offended by its owner, it turns on them.

action->Sense Companion.name: Sense Companion
action->Sense Companion.description: A scorned hound knows the direction of its past owner or family (as long as they're both on the same plane), but not the distance.

action->Stench.name: Stench
action->Stench.description: 15 feet. A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:14] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Indispuesto 1} (plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Lentificado 1} for as long as it's sickened on a critical failure). While within the aura, affected creatures take a -2 circumstance penalty to saves against disease and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

action->Betray the Pack.name: Betray the Pack
action->Betray the Pack.description: The hound's Strikes deal 1d4 additional damage to creatures that were once its owner, companion, fellow pet, family, household, or pack.

Seetangeist.name: Seetangeist
Seetangeist.publicNotes:
Seetangeists are composed of the bloated corpses of marine life, encrusted with bleached coral and wrapped in rotting seaweed. Although occasionally created by water-breathing necromancers, seetangeists most often arise when an aquatic ecosystem collapses, resulting in the deaths of countless creatures. Bound by spiritual trauma, these corpses merge together, forming a writhing school of flesh and decaying plant matter that seeks out and consumes what life remains in the region.

The disasters that lead to the creation of seetangeists are typically caused by mortal interference: overhunting, ecological overexploitation, pollution, or necromantic magic and experimentation.

When disaster befalls the natural world, a different kind of undead may rise, entirely unlike risen humanoids. Most plants, fish, insects, and other animals don't possess a psychology complex enough to experience the emotional catharsis required to return as undead without intervention from an outside force. Mass extinction can serve as that outside force.

Humanoids clear-cutting an entire forest or a seaside community wiping out a species critical to their ecosystem can spawn a verdorite. The collective spiritual energy and emotional distress of a dying biome powers these undead, formed as conglomerates or representatives.

action->Necrotic Runoff.name: Necrotic Runoff
action->Necrotic Runoff.description: 60 feet. A seetangeist secretes foul fluids into the surrounding water, poisoning it. A creature that enters or starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:29] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Indispuesto 2} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Indispuesto 3} on a critical failure). Creatures that breathe water take a -2 circumstance penalty to this saving throw. As long as a creature remains in the aura, it can't reduce its sickened condition below 1. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

action->Putrid Assault.name: Putrid Assault
action->Putrid Assault.description: Each enemy in the seetangeist's space takes @Damage[3d6[slashing],3d6[poison]]{3d6 slashing and 3d6 poison damage} (@Check[type:reflex|dc:32|basic:true]). A creature that fails this save also takes @Damage[1d12[persistent,poison]].

Shadern Immolator.name: Inmolador carbonizado
Shadern Immolator.publicNotes:
Los mortics goblins tienen la carne ennegrecida y carbonizada y quemaduras latentes por todo su cuerpo, como si los hubieran quemado vivos. Son altamente inflamables, pero están habituados al dolor y disfrutan incendiándose a sí mismos (y a todo lo demás).

Los mortics son humanoides abrumados por la energía del vacío que se aferran tenazmente a la vida, manteniéndose en la delgada línea entre la vida y la muerte.

action->Consecration Vulnerability.name: Vulnerabilidad a la consagración
action->Consecration Vulnerability.description: Un carbonizado que está en una zona de culto dedicada a una dios no maligno o en suelo sagrado, como un área bendecida por @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.y3jlOfgsQH1JjkJE]{Terreno santificado}, queda @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Lentificado 1}.

action->Smoke Vision.name: Visión en el humo
action->Smoke Vision.description: El carbonizado ignora el estado @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Oculto} debido al humo.

action->Death Gasp.name: Bocanada de la muerte
action->Death Gasp.description:
El carbonizado da una gran bocanada de aire y la aguanta, suspendiendo temporalmente sus procesos biológicos y convirtiéndose en muerto viviente. El carbonizado obtiene los rasgos de muerto viviente y se vuelve inmune a dormir, efectos de muerte, enfermedad, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Parálisis}, sangrado y veneno. Cualquier efecto de este tipo que le está afectando en este momento queda suspendido hasta que vuelve a respirar.

Bocanada de muerte dura mientras el carbonizado contiene la respiración (hasta 8 asaltos).

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.lyeRL7khixdlJsaf]{Efecto: Bocanada de muerte}

action->Exhale.name: Exhalación
action->Exhale.description: El carbonizado exhala una nube de humo denso en una emanación de 10 pies (3 m) centrada en él, que permanece en su lugar [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 asaltos}. Todas las criaturas en la nube quedan @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Ocultas}, y todas las criaturas fuera de la nube quedan ocultas para las que están en su interior. Exhalación acaba Bocanada de muerte, puesto que el carbonizado deja de contener la respiración.

action->Smoldering Fist.name: Puño incandescente
action->Smoldering Fist.description: Con un crítico, el Golpe de puño del carbonizado también causa 1d4 persistente por fuego.

Shattered Window.name: Shattered Window
Shattered Window.description: The anger trapped within a structure shatters a window, showering glass in adjacent spaces.
Shattered Window.disable: @Check[type:occultism|dc:19|name:Suppress the Spiritual Energy] or @Check[type:religion|dc:19|name:Suppress the Spiritual Energy] (trained) to suppress the spiritual energy or @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:20|name:Soothe the Latent Anger] (trained) to soothe the latent anger
Shattered Window.stealth: (trained) to notice cracks spider-webbing across the window

action->Shatter.name: Shatter
action->Shatter.description:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the window

Effect The window shatters, dealing @Damage[(2d6+5)[slashing]] damage to creatures within 5 feet (@Check[type:reflex|dc:17|basic:true]).

Shredskin.name: Shredskin
Shredskin.publicNotes: Shredskins are flying, flapping animate skins who wander the lands lamenting their fate and feeling hollow and adrift, as if they're missing something crucial inside them. They try to enshroud and seize humanoid bodies—either corpses or living creatures, anything to fill that void within themselves. Though many will take any body they can overpower, some know who created them and desire only that body to claim as their own, the truest fulfillment of their accursed nature. Shredskins come from people skinned as inhumane trophies, or who were killed in a gruesome way that left only their skin intact, such as being pulled into a machine and degloved.

action->Compression.name: Compression
action->Compression.description: When a shredskin successfully @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.kMcV8e5EZUxa6evt]{Squeezes}, it moves through the tight space at full speed. Narrow confines are not difficult terrain for a shredskin.

action->Constrict.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.g26YiEIfSHCpLocV]

@Damage[(1d6+2)[bludgeoning]], @Check[type:fortitude|dc:16|basic:true]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.Constrict]

action->Control Body.name: Control Body
action->Control Body.description:
Requirements The shredskin is in the same space as a Medium or Small humanoid-shaped corpse

Effect The shredskin wraps itself around the corpse as a new skin and takes control of the host body. While controlling a host, the shredskin uses the host's Speed but its own attacks, and it loses its Grab and Enshroud abilities. Attacks that target the shredskin while it controls a body deal half of the damage to the shredskin and half of the damage to the host body. Attacks that target an area deal damage to both the shredskin and host body normally. The shredskin can release the host body as a free action at the start of its turn. If the host is destroyed, the shredskin automatically releases the body and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Desprevenido} for 1 round. A Medium corpse typically has 15 Hit Points, while a Small corpse typically has 10 Hit Points. Creatures can notice that a corpse is controlled by a shredskin by succeeding at a Perception check against the shredskin's Deception DC (typically 18).

action->Enshroud.name: Enshroud
action->Enshroud.description:
Requirements The shredskin is restraining a creature

Effect The shredskin wraps itself around the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Neutralizado} creature like a shirt, seizing control. The shredskin rolls an @Check[type:athletics|defense:fortitude] check against the creature's Fortitude DC. On a success, the creature becomes restrained until it Escapes and the shredskin can control the creature as described in Control Body. At the start of each of its turns, the shredskin can attempt to Constrict the enshrouded creature as a free action. An enshrouded creature can attempt a DC 16 Escape check to break free (DC 18 if the shredskin critically succeeded its Athletics check to Enshroud).

Siabrae.name: Siabrae
Siabrae.publicNotes:
A siabrae is a determined and merciless foe, bound by a twisted sense of duty to nature so strong that it drove them to commit unspeakable acts and arise as an undead menace.

A siabrae is an abomination. A skeletal druid or wild witch, with bones the texture of stone and antlers of rock rising from their rough skulls, they're people who have willingly paid the ultimate price to protect the natural world. It rarely goes well for them.

Druids and wild witches protect Golarion's environment from a host of threats—demonic invasions and undead horrors, otherworldly corruptions, and mundane greed and cupidity. Sometimes, they're tested beyond what they can bear, faced with forces they're incapable of defeating. Such a druid, seeing everything they have protected fall to destruction, might try the unthinkable. They might perform the ritual known as Welcome the Blighted Soul.

With desperate prayer and a gory blood sacrifice, the druid becomes a siabrae, taking the corruption of the land into themself and binding both together, so they might survive and fight monsters in nature's service. A small few are able to use their newfound power to save their lands, and so the ritual's legend grows. Much more often, the druid becomes a twisted guardian of something past saving, the land they protect a macabre parody of healthy nature. Most siabraes are proud and bitter, obsessed with duty and branding their living brethren as cowards and traitors.

spell->Dancing Lights.name: Dancing Lights
spell->Dancing Lights.description: You create up to four floating lights, no two of which are more than 10 feet apart. Each sheds light like a torch. When you Sustain the Spell, you can move any number of lights up to 60 feet. Each light must remain within 120 feet of you and within 10 feet of all others, or it winks out.

spell->Produce Flame.name: Produce Flame
spell->Produce Flame.description:
A small ball of flame appears in the palm of your hand, and you lash out with it either in melee or at range. Make a spell attack roll against your target's AC. This is normally a ranged attack, but you can also make a melee attack against a creature in your unarmed reach.

On a success, you deal 2d4 fire damage. On a critical success, the target takes double damage and @Damage[(@item.level)d4[persistent,fire]] damage.

Heightened (+1) Increase the damage by 1d4 and the persistent damage on a critical hit by 1d4.

weapon->Staff of Nature's Vengeance.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.aPbDAb6cJKPvCHk2]

This stout staff is made from gnarled hawthorn. When used as a weapon, a staff of nature's vengeance permanently has the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.s3abwDbTV43pGFFW]{Shillelagh}

Activate Cast a Spell

Effect You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.

Craft Requirements Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells.

  • Cantrip @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.uZK2BYzPnxUBnDjr]{Tangle Vine}
  • 1st @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.r3NeUnsgt9mS03Sn]{Shocking Grasp}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.DYdvMZ8G2LiSLVWw]{Spider Sting}
  • 2nd @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.2ZdHjnpEQJuqOYSG]{Floating Flame}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.cB17yFc9456Pyfec]{Vomit Swarm}
  • 3rd @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gPvtmKMRpg9I9D7H]{Earthbind}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.KsWhliKfUs3IpW3c]{Wall of Thorns}
  • 4th @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.VuPDHoVEPLbMfCJC]{Bestial Curse}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.3xD8DYrr8YDVYGg7]{Spike Stones}

consumable->Scroll of True Seeing (Level.name: Scroll of True Seeing (Level 6)
consumable->Scroll of True Seeing (Level.consumableSpellDescription: You see things within 60 feet as they actually are. The GM rolls a secret counteract check against any illusion or transmutation in the area, but only for the purpose of determining whether you see through it (for instance, if the check succeeds against a polymorph spell, you can see the creature's true form, but you don't end the polymorph spell).
consumable->Scroll of True Seeing (Level.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.4sGIy77COooxhQuC]

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.uqlxMQQeSGWEVjki]{Truesight}

Casting a Spell from a scroll requires holding the scroll in one hand and activating it with a Cast a Spell activity using the normal number of actions for that spell.

To Cast a Spell from a scroll, the spell must appear on your spell list. Because you're the one Casting the Spell, use your spell attack roll and spell DC. The spell also gains the appropriate trait for your tradition (arcane, divine, occult, or primal).

Any physical material components and costs are provided when a scroll is created, so you don't need to provide them when Casting a Spell from a scroll. You must replace any required material component for that spell with a somatic component. If the spell requires a focus, you must have that focus to Cast the Spell from a scroll.

Note: To create a scroll or wand of a specific spell, drag the spell from the compendium or compendium browser into the inventory of a PC, NPC, or loot actor.

action->Miasma.name: Miasma
action->Miasma.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.Es8g7kZrLAuNdiD1]

20 foot, @Check[type:fortitude|dc:37]{DC equal to the siabrae's spell DC - 4}.

A creature that enters the aura or begins its turn there becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Indispuesto 2} on a failure (or sickened 4 on a critical failure). An animal, fey, or plant that rolls a failure gets a critical failure instead. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to the siabrae's miasma for 1 minute.

action->Steady Spellcasting.name: Steady Spellcasting
action->Steady Spellcasting.description: If a reaction would disrupt the siabrae's spellcasting action, the siabrae attempts a @Check[type:flat|dc:15]. On a success, the action isn't disrupted.

action->Stony Shards.name: Stony Shards
action->Stony Shards.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-family-ability-glossary.Item.WUXY9F8SLVQSC2b8]

Tiny shards break off the siabrae's antlers when they attack, lodging in the target's wounds and inflicting a terrible curse. A creature damaged by a siabrae's stone antlers Strike must succeed at a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:37] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Torpe 2} for [[/r 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds} on a failure. If the creature critically fails, or fails while already clumsy 2 or greater, the creature is petrified.

Silent Stalker.name: Silent Stalker
Silent Stalker.languages: any one spoken in life by their murderer (typically common; can't speak)
Silent Stalker.publicNotes:
When a person is betrayed and killed in a way that their voice was silenced (such as through strangulation by a lover or close confidante), they might come back as a silent stalker. A silent stalker is a horrifying, twisted likeness of the person they were in life, with one obvious difference: the silent stalker has no mouth, simply a smooth patch of flesh where a mouth should be. Sound is suppressed in an area around a silent stalker, but once someone notices the quiet, it's often too late.

A revenant is a vengeful undead who stalks the one who killed them in life. They're relentless in the pursuit of their murderer, seeking a final justice.

action->Self-Loathing.name: Self-Loathing
action->Self-Loathing.description:
If a silent stalker sees their own reflection or any object important to them in life, they must attempt a @Check[type:will|dc:33] save.

Critical Success The pale stranger is unaffected and can no longer be affected by that reflection or object in this way.

Success The silent stalker is distracted by self-loathing and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Lentificado 1} for 1 round.

Failure The silent stalker becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinado} by the source of their self-loathing and does everything they can to destroy it until the end of their next turn.

Critical Failure The silent stalker becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Inmovilizado} as long as the source of their self-loathing is apparent, until they're attacked, or until they see their murderer.

action->Sense Murderer.name: Sense Murderer
action->Sense Murderer.description: A silent stalker knows the direction of their murderer (as long as both are on the same plane), but not the distance.

action->Silent Aura.name: Silent Aura
action->Silent Aura.description:
10 feet. A silent stalker makes no sound, preventing creatures from noticing them using hearing or a similar sense alone. This silence stops all sound within it or passing through. The silent stalker and all creatures in the aura can't use sonic attacks or actions that have the auditory trait; this prevents creatures from casting spells with verbal components or activating items with command components.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.YKCsmlMgI0aS7joO]{Effect: Silent Aura}

action->Undying Vendetta.name: Undying Vendetta
action->Undying Vendetta.description: If the silent stalker's murderer dies, the pale stranger is immediately destroyed. A pale stranger that can't sense their murderer must attempt a @Check[type:flat|dc:11] once every 24 hours to avoid becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Inmovilizado} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Tumbado}; they immediately rise again once they can sense their murderer. A murderer who becomes undead does not trigger the silent stalker's destruction until the murderer is finally destroyed. The silent stalker gains a +2 status bonus to checks and DCs against their murderer.

action->Implacable Advance.name: Implacable Advance
action->Implacable Advance.description: The silent stalker Strides twice and makes a claw Strike; they can ignore difficult terrain during these Strides if they advance toward their murderer. If they Strike and Grab their murderer this turn, they can Constrict as a free action immediately after the Grab.

Siphoning Spirit.name: Siphoning Spirit
Siphoning Spirit.description: A formless spirit drains life from the living, becoming progressively visible as its victims weaken.
Siphoning Spirit.disable: @Check[type:occultism|dc:48] (expert) or @Check[type:religion|dc:48] (expert) to weaken the spirit; four total successes are required to disable the haunt
Siphoning Spirit.reset: After 1 minute, the spirit is satiated, and the haunt deactivates as it returns to a resting state. Over the course of 1 hour, the spirit's siphoned life energy disperses and the haunt resets.
Siphoning Spirit.routine:
(1 action; death, divine, necromancy, void) The spirit inhales, siphoning the souls of the living and becoming more visible. Each creature within @Template[type:burst|distance:60]{60 feet} takes @Damage[(4d10+20)[void]] damage, with a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:41|traits:death,divine,necromancy,void,damaging-effect] save. If no creatures are within the area, the haunt moves up to 120 feet into the largest concentration of living creatures and then inhales.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage and increases its @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drenado} value by 1, to a maximum of drained 4.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and increases its drained value by 2, to a maximum of drained 4. If the creature was already drained 4, it dies.

action->Sudden Siphon.name: Sudden Siphon
action->Sudden Siphon.description:
Trigger A living creature passes within 15 feet of the spirit

Effect The invisible spirit latches onto the life essence of the living, dealing @Damage[(4d10+20)[void]] damage to all living creatures within @Template[type:burst|distance:60]{60 feet} (@Check[type:fortitude|dc:41|basic:true] save). The haunt then rolls initiative.

Skeletal Mage.name: Skeletal Mage
Skeletal Mage.publicNotes:
Rarely, the bones of a spellcaster can be used to create a skeleton that retains a limited ability to cast spells. These spells are almost always offensive in nature, and the skeletal mage uses them with reckless abandon. Skeletal mages are often misidentified as liches, and while not nearly as cunning or deadly, they still pose a significant threat to their foes.

Among the ranks of the dead, none are so numerous, nor so varied, as the skeleton. While most are almost entirely made from bone, some maintain a few scraps of flesh to aid them in movement, such as wing membranes.

spell->Produce Flame.name: Produce Flame
spell->Produce Flame.description:
A small ball of flame appears in the palm of your hand, and you lash out with it either in melee or at range. Make a spell attack roll against your target's AC. This is normally a ranged attack, but you can also make a melee attack against a creature in your unarmed reach.

On a success, you deal 2d4 fire damage. On a critical success, the target takes double damage and @Damage[(@item.level)d4[persistent,fire]] damage.

Heightened (+1) Increase the damage by 1d4 and the persistent damage on a critical hit by 1d4.

Skeletal Soldier.name: Skeletal Soldier
Skeletal Soldier.publicNotes:
These soldiers continue to follow orders in death, still wielding the armaments they spent a lifetime learning how to use. While frequently made from the bones of humans or dwarves, other types of humanoids are quite common, such as bugbears, hobgoblins, and orcs. Often, these soldiers wield the weapons most common among their people instead of those listed here.

Among the ranks of the dead, none are so numerous, nor so varied, as the skeleton. While most are almost entirely made from bone, some maintain a few scraps of flesh to aid them in movement, such as wing membranes.

armor->Chain Shirt.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.MPcM4Wt6KmWE2kGL]

Sometimes called a hauberk, this is a long shirt constructed of the same metal rings as chainmail. However, it is much lighter than chainmail and protects only the torso, upper arms, and upper legs of its wearer.

action->Set Defense.name: Set Defense
action->Set Defense.description: The skeletal soldier sets its glaive (or other reach weapon) to defend the area around it. It gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.hFtNbo1LKYCoDy2O]{Attack of Opportunity} reaction for that weapon only. This lasts until the skeleton takes an action with the move or attack trait or is destroyed.

Sluagh Reaper.name: Sluagh Reaper
Sluagh Reaper.publicNotes:
Most sluagh are reapers: powerful killers subservient to their queen. After they collect souls on a hunt, they dutifully return their plunder to the queen. Though quite clever, they're inscrutable, lacking the whimsy and sociability that characterize many living fey. They do show merriment on occasion though, giggling as their claws draw closer to their quarry's throat.

A soul about to leave its physical body gives off a peculiar scent, usually unnoticed by mortal creatures. But the undead fey called sluagh can smell and track down its location with frightening ease. Unlike most fey, sluagh are undead, possessing strong connections to void energy and mortal souls. Sluagh collect the souls of those close to death and bring them to feed their queen.

Most sluagh appear bald-headed, with rounded ears, sharp teeth, glossy black eyes, and scrawny bodies. Their rib cages show through their skin, which is translucent and soft as velvet.

Sluagh can become ghostly and incorporeal for brief periods. They use this power to stalk their prey to great effect. Sometimes the glimpse of a sluagh is mistaken for a ghost sighting.

action->Frightful Presence.description:

30 feet. @Check[type:will|dc:26]

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.FrightfulPresence]

action->Soulscent (Imprecise) 100 feet.name: Soulscent (Imprecise) 100 feet
action->Soulscent (Imprecise) 100 feet.description: The sluagh is aware of all @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Condenado} creatures, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{Moribundo} creatures, and creatures that died within the last hour within the listed range.

action->Ghostly Swoop.name: Ghostly Swoop
action->Ghostly Swoop.description:
The sluagh reaper becomes incorporeal until the start of their next turn, and Flies up to their fly Speed. While incorporeal, they are immune to precision damage, and have resistance 10 to all damage (except force, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.JQdwHECogcTzdd8R]{Ghost Touch}, or vitality); this resistance is doubled against non-magical damage.

After using Ghostly Swoop, the sluagh reaper can't use it again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Ghostly Swoop]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Sneak Attack.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.AWvNPE4U0kEJSL1T]

The sluagh reaper deals an additional 3d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Desprevenido} creatures.

action->Steal Soul.name: Steal Soul
action->Steal Soul.description:
The sluagh reaper touches a dying creature or a creature that died within the past 1 hour. If the target is a dying creature, it must attempt a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:28] save-on a failure, its dying value increases by 1 (2 on a critical failure). If the target is dead, it receives no save and the sluagh imprisons its soul in a small, grimy sack that keeps stolen souls fresh.

The sack is magically locked. Any sluagh can open it without difficulty, but any others must @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.2EE4aF4SZpYf0R6H]{Pick the Lock} (@Check[type:thievery|dc:35|traits:action:pick-a-lock]) or use @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (5th level; counteract DC 32). A creature can Interact to empty an unlocked sack, which frees the souls but doesn't return them to life. While a soul is imprisoned, it can't be brought back to life by any means short of a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.6dDtGIUerazSHIOu]{Wish} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.YfJTXyVGzLhM6V8U]{Miracle}. The sack can hold 12 souls at a time. Sluagh reapers typically attempt to return the souls to a queen so she can devour them.
